titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 624

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=43 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-13 14:14:00.588392+00 | translation moa=44 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-13 14:14:00.541237+00

div.p:
Alex’ . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . Clericis et Laicis 
Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri nos concessisse . et hac Carta nostra confirmasse illam resig
nationem et quietam clamationem quam Patricius filius Comitis . Patricii . fecit deo et
Beate Marie . et Sancto Cuthberto . et Sancte Abbe . et Monachis Dunolmensibus apud . Coldigham .
deo seruientibus de villa de Swinewude cum hominibus et iustis pertinentiis suis . Tenend’ .
ita libere et quiete . Sicut Carta predicti Patricii . et Confirmatio Comitis . Patricii . patris
ipsius testantur . Saluo seruicio nostro . Testibus . Herberto . Abbate de Calchou . Waltero . Olifard’ . Ius
tic’ . Laodonie . Ingelramo . de Balliol’ . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . dauid . Marescallo . Iohanne Giffard’ .
Bernardo . de Hauden’ . Heruico . Marescallo . Apud Rokesburg’ . ultimo die Nouembris .
Anno regni nostri Octauo .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Let present and future know that we have granted, and by this our charter made firm the resignation and quitclaim that Patrick son of Earl Patrick made to God and to Blessed Mary, and to Saint Cuthbert, and to Saint Æbbe, and to the monks of Durham serving God at Coldingham, of the toun of Swinwood with its men and just pertinents, to be held as freely and quietly as the charter of the aforesaid Patrick and the Confirmation of Earl Patrick, his father, declare, Saving our service. As witnesses: Herbert, Abbot of Kelso, Walter Oliphant, Justiciar of Lothian, Ingram de Balliol, John of Maxwell, David the Marshal, John Gifford, Bernard of Howden, Hervey the Marshal. At Roxburgh, the last day of November, the Eighth Year of our reign.
